content.text:
Now I can clearly see the molecule structure in the image. Let me identify atom index 5 (C:5) and its neighbor atoms.

From the image, I can locate atom C:5 (carbon, index 5) which is an orange sphere labeled "C:5" in the center-left portion of the molecule. Looking at the covalent bonds connected to C:5, I can see:

- C:5 is bonded to N:4 (blue sphere, nitrogen, index 4)
- C:5 is bonded to O:6 (red sphere, oxygen, index 6)  
- C:5 is bonded to C:11 (orange sphere, carbon, index 11)

These three atoms are directly connected to C:5 via covalent bonds, as shown by the yellow connecting rods.

The geomconf_index for this molecule is 8978, as indicated by the filename "geomconf_8978.xyz".

So the neighbor atoms of atom index 5 (C) are:
- Atom index 4, element N
- Atom index 6, element O
- Atom index 11, element C

question: Please find the neighbour atoms of the atom with atomic index 5, whose element should be C. Here, neighbour atoms are defined as atoms that are covalently connected to this atom. Report all neighbour atoms by listing their atomic index (as shown in the image) and their element. Also return the geomconf_index for this molecule.
